# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:18-slim

# Set environment variables
ENV NODE_ENV=production

# Create and set the working directory
WORKDIR /usr/src/app

# Install system utilities
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    curl \
    git \
    vim \
    && apt-get clean && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json
COPY package*.json ./

# Install Node.js dependencies
RUN npm install --only=production && npm cache clean --force

# Copy application source code
COPY . .

# Set the user to node for security
USER node

# Expose the application port
EXPOSE 3000

# Start the application
CMD ["node", "app.js"]
